mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 3.0% signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 3.0% and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.41% suggests easing environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that increases recession risk, causing equity investors to reduce risk exposure and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% and sustains above this level for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.8% is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) combined with falling housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines 2% month-over-month AND HOUST declines 5% month-over-month simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 505969M and HOUST at 1162K units
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the joint signal has Rank IC < 0.05 when tested independently against single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 2% year-over-year signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and productivity gains, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window, or the directional accuracy is below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerating below 100K new jobs per month combined with rising unemployment signals labor market inflection point, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change falls below 100K AND UNRATE rises 0.2pp or more in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 150816K and UNRATE at 3.8%
regime_hint: labor market inflection or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term adds less than 0.03 to Rank IC compared to single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerating above 3.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 3.0% signals real rate compression and monetary policy lag, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as real asset values compress.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS is below 3.0% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 254.148 and FEDFUNDS at 2.41%
regime_hint: stagflationary pressure or monetary policy lag regime
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or the real rate compression signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerating to below 1.5% annualized rate signals late-cycle exhaustion and earnings growth deceleration, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as terminal growth expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 1.5% in latest available quarter
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and forward SPX returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerating above 1.3M annualized units signals strong residential investment demand and forward consumption expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as cyclical sector earnings expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 1.3M annualized units and sustains above this level for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between HOUST levels and 5-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 1-year rolling window.
